results.title: Kriti Srivastava
highlights:
Manager - AI ML, Analytics & Data Engg Programs at Lennox
...
I bring over 10 years of Data Industry experience, including 7+ years focused on AI and machine learning, leading complex programs and initiatives across diverse domains such as sales, marketing, finance, supply chain, and product. I specialize in end-to-end process and program management with a strong technical foundation as an AI/ML practitioner and 0->1 Product Development. My approach bridges strategy, execution, and technology to deliver scalable, data-driven solutions that create measurable business value. A key highlight in my career was leading the development and launch of a customer-centric Generative AI chatbot for a global manufacturing organization — improving operational efficiency, customer engagement, and frontline productivity. This involved: • Driving cross-functional collaboration among data science, product, and engineering teams • Implementing responsible AI principles and ensuring explainability • Integrating advanced ML capabilities with real-time industrial and customer workflows I also hold 5 US patents in AI/ML technologies, underscoring my commitment to innovation and advancing the state of the art. # Demand Planner – Factory Service Parts
...
The Demand Planner – Factory Service Parts is responsible for driving performance, efficiency, and strategic growth in our parts business. This role oversees PIPO (Phase In/Phase Out) processes, inventory management, and demand planning to ensure we meet customer demand while optimizing resources. The ideal candidate will bring a strategic mindset, deep technical knowledge of parts management, and the ability to lead initiatives that improve the customer experience and strengthen our market position.
...
- Develop and maintain statistical forecasts for service parts using advanced statistical models).
- Incorporate product life cycle curves, seasonality, and intermittent demand patterns into forecasting.
- Monitor forecast accuracy and continuously improve models and improve forecast BIAS/MAPE
...
- Analyze failure rates and usage patterns to predict spare parts demand.
- Understand attachment rates of parts to OEM assemblies and final products.
- Collaborate with engineering and reliability teams to integrate failure data into planning.
...
- Determine safety stock and reorder points based on service level targets and lead times.
- Manage end-of-life and obsolescence planning for parts.
...
closely with procurement,
...
- Provide regular reports on forecast accuracy, inventory health, and service level performance.
- Use ERP and planning tools (SAP, IBP, Oracle, or similar) for data analysis and execution.
...
- Experience improving forecast accuracy, inventory turnover, and OTIF performance
- Ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders and present insights to leadership
- Proven track record of turning complex data into clear, actionable recommendations
...
Compensation: This is a salaried exempt role. The starting salary range for this role and market is between $64,000-$84,000 annually. Factors that may affect starting salary include geography/market and the skills, education, experience, and other qualifications of the successful candidate. Employees in this role are also eligible for an annual bonus in accordance with the terms of the Company’s applicable plan. Employees in this role are not eligible for overtime.

...
Lennox (NYSE: LII) Driven by 130 years of legacy, HVAC and refrigeration success, Lennox provides our residential and commercial customers with industry-leading climate-control solutions. At Lennox, we win as a team, aiming for excellence and delivering innovative, sustainable products and services. Our culture guides us and creates a workplace where all employees feel heard and welcomed. Lennox is a global community that values each team member’s contributions and offers a supportive environment for career development. Come, stay, and grow with us.
...
Allied Air Enterprises is a division of Lennox International. We bring together the collective strengths of seven comfort brands –
...
the ultimate source for heating and cooling solutions. From compact residential applications to large commercial projects, Allied Aire Enterprises redefines ease-of-business through breadth of product, resources and competencies unparalleled in the market.
...
The Quality Engineer III is responsible for driving product, process, and supplier quality improvements across manufacturing operations. This role applies advanced quality engineering principles, statistical analysis, problem-solving methodologies, and Quality Management System (QMS) expertise to improve product reliability, reduce variation, ensure compliance with ISO standards, and support supplier quality performance. The Quality Engineer III serves as a key technical resource for production, engineering, operations, and suppliers while leading continuous improvement initiatives and mentoring less experienced team members.
...
- Lead root cause investigations and implementation of corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) using methodologies such as 8D, DMAIC, and structured problem solving.
...
- Apply statistical tools including SPC, Cp/Cpk studies, hypothesis testing, control charts, and capability analysis to drive data-based decision making.
...
Conduct Measurement System Analysis
...
- Lead and support Six Sigma and continuous improvement projects focused on reducing waste, variation, and cost.
...
- Maintain and improve the Quality Management System in accordance with ISO 9001 requirements.
...
and quality standards.
...
### What We Are Looking For
...
Experience with:
...
- Root Cause Analysis
- Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA)
- SPC and Capability Analysis (Cp/Cpk)
- PFMEA and Control Plans
- PPAP and Supplier Quality Management
- MSA and Gage R&R
- 8D Problem Solving
- Certified ISO 9001 Lead Auditor Quality Management Systems
...
- Strong communication, project management, and cross-functional collaboration skills.
- ASQ Certified Quality Engineer (CQE), Certified Manager of Quality/Organizational Excellence (CMQ/OE), or equivalent certification.
- Lean Six Sigma Green Belt or Black Belt certification.
- Certified ISO 9001:2015 supplier audits and supplier development initiatives.
- Experience with DOE, ANOVA, and advanced statistical methods.
- Knowledge of ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems.
...
Compensation: This is a salaried exempt role. The starting salary range for this role and market is between $91,000 - $120,000 annually. Factors that may affect starting salary include geography/market and the skills, education, experience, and other qualifications of the successful candidate. Employees in this role are also eligible for an annual bonus in accordance with the terms of the Company’s applicable plan. Employees in this role are not eligible for overtime.

# Applied Data Scientist
...
Are you passionate about using data science, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics to propel decisions? If you are interested in driving meaningful change for a growth focused consumer products brand, this is an opportunity to join the market-leading Delta Faucet Company team as we use analytics to turbocharge strategic insights!
...
Reporting to the Manager of Data Science and Analytics, this role will work with analytics and domain specialists across the business to deliver high-impact insights and data science solutions. You will develop advanced analytical models, experimentation tests, applied AI techniques, and partner with business stakeholders to translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations that support the company’s growth initiatives.
...
As an Applied Data Scientist within Delta Faucet Company’s Analytics Center of Excellence (ACoE), you will act as an owner of high impact analysis and data science initiatives that support complex, ambiguous business decisions. This role focuses on advanced analytics, experimentation, and applied AI.
...
- Independently lead end-to-end analytics and data science projects, from problem framing through delivery of actionable insights.
- Apply statistical methods, machine learning, and analytical modeling to solve business problems related to growth, customer behavior, market dynamics, and operational efficiency.
- Develop and maintain analytical data models and curated datasets, partnering with data engineering and BI teams.
- Build AI and machine learning solutions that are aligned with business needs.
- Collaborate closely with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into clear recommendations.
- Present data-driven recommendations to leaders across the organization, including tactical and C-suite.
...
- 4+ years of experience in data science, advanced analytics, or applied machine learning roles.
- 4+ years of hands-on experience applying statistical methods and analytical modeling to real business problems.
- 4+ years of experience using Python for data analysis, modeling, and experimentation.
- 4+ years of experience writing and refining complex SQL queries against large, structured datasets.
- 3+ years of experience partnering directly with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into actionable recommendations.
- 2+ years of experience working in a modern analytics platform (e.g., Databricks, Spark/PySpark, or similar).
- 1+ years of hands-on‑ experience applying generative AI technologies (e.g., LLM APIs, task‑specific GenAI workflows) in an enterprise analytics environment.
...
- Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Data Science, Economics, or a related quantitative field.
...
- 2+ years of experience with analytics engineering and data modeling, including building reusable, well-documented analytical datasets; familiarity with medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) preferred.
- 1+ years of experience with model monitoring, experiment tracking, and model evaluation in production settings.
- 1+ years of experience with conjoint analysis or other stated-preference research methods.

results.title: Lead Data Scientist Job in Boston | Lennar Homes
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist Job in Boston | Lennar Homes
...
# Lead Data Scientist
...
Lennar Homes
...
- We require a bachelors or masters degree in Statistics, Economics, Math, Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience. - We need 5+ years of relevant experience as a data scientist, machine learning engineer, or applied AI developer delivering production-ready models and systems; candidates with a PhD need 1+ year, and those with an MS need 3+ years. - We expect strong proficiency in Python and SQL, plus experience owning the full data science stack from data pipelines through model deployment. - We look for hands-on experience with AI development frameworks such as LangChain, Strands, Amazon Bedrock, AgentCore, or similar tools. - We require experience with experimentation methods including A/B testing, uplift modeling, multi-armed bandits, and causal machine learning. - We value exposure to machine vision techniques such as CNNs, transfer learning, and embeddings, as well as NLP methods including embeddings, transformers, and prompt engineering. - We expect familiarity with AI agent observability and evaluation tools such as LangFuse, RAGAS, Weights & Biases, or custom monitoring solutions. - We need experience integrating systems through APIs, A2A protocol, MCP servers, and orchestration pipelines. - We look for comfort working with large-scale, imperfect real-world data and making progress in complex environments. - We require strong engineering capability to design and maintain production pipelines, microservices, and scalable systems. - We value a proven ability to navigate ambiguity, prototype quickly, and move solutions into production. - We need a collaborative communicator who can align technical solutions with business priorities across varied stakeholders. - Bonus: we consider experience with RAG pipelines, LLM fine-tuning, RLHF, multi-agent orchestration, feature stores, survival analysis or churn modeling, and attribution modeling an advantage. - This is primarily a sedentary office role that requires the ability to operate computer equipment and perform physical movements such as speaking, hearing, bending, stooping, reaching, lifting, and carrying up to 25 lbs, with finger dexterity required.
...
We are Lennar, one of the nations leading homebuilders and a Fortune 500 company, focused on creating exceptional experiences for our homeowners, communities, and associates through quality homes, strong customer service, and community involvement. In this Lead Data Scientist role, we give you the opportunity to shape how we price, sell, and personalize customer experiences across more than 40 divisions by building advanced models and AI agents end to end, from research through production monitoring. We offer a supportive environment with comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, and vision coverage; a 401(k) with company match; paid parental leave; education assistance; adoption assistance; vacation, holiday, sick, and personal time; referral bonuses; home purchase discounts; and more. We also foster growth, innovation, and collaboration while complying with equal opportunity and fair employment practices.

...
Title: AI Engineer - Digital Transformation Marketing & Sales
...
Lenovo is a US$69 billion revenue global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers every day in 180 markets. Focused on a bold vision to deliver Smarter Technology for All, Lenovo has built on its success as the world's largest PC company with a full-stack portfolio of AI-enabled, AI-ready, and AI-optimized devices (PCs, workstations, smartphones, tablets), infrastructure (server, storage, edge, high performance computing and software defined infrastructure), software, solutions, and services. Lenovo's continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. Lenovo is listed on the Hong Kong stock exchange under Lenovo Group Limited (HKSE: 992) (ADR: LNVGY).

We are seeking an AI Engineer to support Lenovo's global Marketing and Sales organizations. In this role, you will lead the development of agentic AI solutions that accelerate Lenovo's digital transformation in the AI era. You will work at the intersection of advanced analytics, enterprise systems, and business strategy to deliver scalable, compliant, and high-impact AI-driven solutions.

Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Software Engineering, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related field.3+ years of hands-on experience designing and developing ML/AI solutions in production environments.
